column1: Holistic Assignment Performance - Completion, Effort, Organization, and Overall Quality (4-point-scale)

This category accounts for the full assignment as a completed product, including but not limited to required sections, prompts, responses, visuals, captions, explanations, examples, artifacts, following directions, care, effort, readability, overall completion, and whether the final product works as a complete assignment. It is not linked to one specific CCSS standard and preserves teacher judgment and grading flexibility.

column2: The assignment is complete, careful, readable, coherent, and strong overall. All required parts are meaningfully completed, and the work shows strong effort, understanding, and follow-through.
column3: The assignment successfully meets expectations overall. Most or all required parts are complete and understandable, with only minor issues in accuracy, detail, organization, or presentation.
column4: The assignment is partially complete or uneven. Some required parts may be missing, rushed, unclear, or only partly accurate, but meaningful work and understanding are present.
column5: The assignment shows limited completion or weak follow-through. Several required parts may be missing, unclear, inaccurate, copied, or difficult to evaluate, but there is some attempt.
column6: The assignment is missing, off-task, or too incomplete to demonstrate meaningful effort or understanding.
